mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing activity and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is always active and does not require a threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production leads corporate profits historically
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward S&P 500 return shows no positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.03) with INDPRO MoM growth over any 3-year rolling window, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 9% indicates labor market stress that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If in months following UNRATE > 9% the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive in more than 55% of cases, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound (below 0.25%), further easing is constrained and the economy remains stagnant, so low rates no longer stimulate stocks but instead signal persistent weakness, producing negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Zero lower bound was binding in 2009-2011
failure_condition: If during periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.25% the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive and statistically significant (t-test p < 0.05), reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicate collapsing consumer demand and income, which directly reduces corporate revenues and earnings, pushing S&P 500 lower over the next month.
failure_condition: If in months with concurrent declines in both series the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of instances, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 500 thousand units monthly, it signals severe weakness in the housing sector that depresses construction activity, consumer wealth, and overall demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If after HOUST < 500 the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive in more than 60% of cases, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% year-on-year triggers expectations of monetary tightening by the Fed, which compresses equity valuations and reduces forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: If following CPI YoY > 3% the 5-day forward S&P 500 return is positive more often than negative (binomial test p > 0.10), reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) decline month-over-month simultaneously, the economy is in a broad-based contraction that severely depresses corporate profits, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM declines and PAYEMS MoM declines
failure_condition: If in months with joint declines the 20-day forward S&P 500 return has a median > 0%, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP (GDPC1) indicates expanding economic output and aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings growth and pushes S&P 500 higher over the following quarter. The signal is always active across all regimes.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward S&P 500 return shows no positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.03) with GDPC1 quarterly growth over any 4-year rolling window, reject hypothesis.
